title: helianthus nuttallii subsp. rydbergii
intro: Helianthus nuttallii subsp. rydbergii is a subspecies of the annual sunflower (Helianthus nuttallii) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is known for its potential medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Helianthus nuttallii subsp. rydbergii include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
